steps.title: Find the mesophotic threshold
steps.content: The mesophotic zone begins at 30 meters below the surface. A coral least suited to full migration there would be the species whose current maximum depth is farthest below 30 meters, since that shows the least evidence of tolerating deeper conditions.

steps.title: Compare maximum depths
steps.content: Choice A uses a minimum depth, which does not show how deep the species can go. Choice C has a 25-meter maximum, close to the threshold. Choice D also uses a minimum depth, which is not the key measure.

steps.title: Select the shallowest maximum
steps.content: The remaining choice identifies Plerogyra discus, whose 15-meter maximum depth is farthest from the mesophotic zone. The answer is B.
